body: Walking through the funnel builder I still cannot tell where to set the conversion window. Any pointers?
Set the conversion window in step settings on the right panel. I've shared a short guide and a ready-made retention funnel template in your workspace.
Could you share a screenshot and the approximate time the redirect loops? That will help us narrow down the SSO config.
Attached the screenshot. It happens right after the Okta redirect, around 9am Mountain.
Thanks for the details, routing the missing-summary issue to the reporting team for review.
This was a timezone setting at the workspace level; I've switched it to your local zone and the dashboards now match.
Confirmed, the times are correct now. Thank you.
Admin invite was blocked by a seat limit on your plan. I've raised the limit; please retry the invite.
Confirmed the discrepancy is from different date ranges on the two charts. Holding while we add a clearer label.
CSV export is now enabled for the analyst role in your workspace. You're all set.
Verified the analysts can export now. Appreciate the quick turnaround.
We've added an index on the largest workspace and load time is down to about 4 seconds. Closing this out.
The ingestion lag was a backlogged queue on our side; it's drained and events are now near real-time.
Happy to add the analyst seat. I've sent an order confirmation for the prorated amount to your billing contact.
I've doubled the reporting endpoint rate limit for your account. Let us know if you still see throttling.
Reproduced the crash on the weekly report screen; a patch is in the next iOS build. Closing once it ships.
The reset was failing because the new password reused a recent one. Cleared it and you should be able to sign in.
The template you shared helped, but two of our event names don't appear in the step picker. Do they need to be in the tracking plan first?
Correct, custom events only show in the step picker once they've been seen at least once or added to the tracking plan. I've added both of yours so they should appear now.
It looks like the affected users aren't yet mapped to a group in your identity provider, which is why they hit the access-denied screen. Can you confirm the group assignment on your end?
